channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: # I tried to trick my own agent into deleting the wrong file 👾
item.description: Month 2 of the AI Red Teaming Journey Month 1 was about building an agent and testing whether it would follow malicious instructions hidden in data. It didn't, twice. Month 2 asks a different...

item.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
item.description: Lawmakers are preparing to introduce an "AI Kill Switch Act" that would require AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security, according to...

item.title: Crucible v1.2 — Interactive TUI, Component Lifecycle Commands, React Native, and Security Hardening
item.description: TL;DR: Crucible is a code generation CLI that scaffolds production-ready components (React / Vue / Angular / React Native) directly into your project. You own every generated file. v1.2 ships a...

item.title: ASP.NET Core Performance Tuning for High-Traffic APIs
item.description: When a .NET API works perfectly in staging and then falls over under real production load, the root cause is almost never a single catastrophic bug. It is usually a collection of small...

item.title: Designing Model-Agnostic Prompts for AI Video Workflows
item.description: AI video tools change quickly. A prompt that works well in one model may behave differently in another, and model-specific syntax can become obsolete after an update. A more durable approach is to...

item.title: The data model I ended up with after trying to publish one post everywhere
item.description: A social post looks like a string until you try to send it to more than one platform. That was roughly where OpenPost started. There was some source content, a list of accounts, and a queue that...

item.title: Day 96/100 – Standard SQL Time Functions in ClickHouse® 26.3: Better ANSI SQL Compatibility
item.description: Introduction One of the key goals of recent ClickHouse® releases has been improving compatibility with the ANSI SQL standard. While ClickHouse has always offered a rich set of date and time...

item.title: Reading 50MB JSONL logs with a viewer in 2026
item.description: Reading 50MB JSONL logs with a viewer in 2026 Use a browser-based JSONL viewer that parses each line as its own JSON object and lays the results out in a filterable, sortable table. That's the...

item.title: Pulseboard: self-hosted HTTP uptime monitoring without the SaaS bill
item.description: Pulseboard: self-hosted HTTP uptime monitoring without the SaaS bill I wanted something like Uptime Kuma — lightweight, self-hosted, easy to run — so I built Pulseboard. What it does HTTP(S)...

item.title: Add "Export as image" to any dashboard
item.description: First published on the Accreditly blog. "Export as image" is one of those features users expect and developers keep putting off. Someone wants to drop a chart into a slide deck or paste a KPI...

item.title: Apple’s OpenAI lawsuit is about who gets to define the post-smartphone era
item.description: Today on Decoder, I’m talking with Hayden Field, The Verge’s senior AI reporter, about the major trade secrets lawsuit between Apple and OpenAI and what this tells us about OpenAI’s future. By now...

item.title: Building a RAG Chatbot with FastAPI and ChromaDB (that runs locally, no API key)
item.description: Everyone wants a chatbot that can answer questions from their own documents — a company handbook, a contract, a research paper. The technique behind this is called RAG (Retrieval-Augmented...

item.title: Ford picks Apple Maps for its next EV platform and updated BlueCruise
item.description: Ford plans to embed Apple Maps directly into its Universal Electric Vehicle Platform, which will start with a $30,000 midsize pickup truck set for release in 2027. In an announcement on Thursday,...

item.title: The sci-fi movie that imagines AI isn’t so dystopian after all
item.description: Imagine you are the parent of an inquisitive seven-year-old who loves his family, playing baseball, and hanging out with his friends. It's the life you've always wanted. And then the worst thing...

item.title: The Download: energy transmission and US threats against Chinese AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The power line that could reshape New York’s grid is...

item.title: How AI helps scientists design the next generation of medicines
item.description: Designing and developing a new medicine is an expensive, failure-prone scientific challenge. A new drug can take many years to develop, at the cost of a significant investment. And even then, most...

item.title: The right-wing boomers protesting data centers have a lot in common with the left
item.description: On a gray, humid Saturday morning in central Florida, a little under a dozen people gathered outside the Spring Hill Branch Library to protest the construction of a hyperscale data center in their...

item.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
item.description: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a...

item.title: Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost...

item.title: End-to-End Encryption and “Going Dark”
item.description: New paper: “Encryption and Globalization 15 Years Later: End-to-End Encryption and the Third Round of the ‘Going Dark’ Debate“: Abstract: This Article updates and expands on 2012 research on...

item.title: Experts say exploiting Anthropic’s Fable isn’t how Kimi K3 got so good
item.description: "I don't think you get a model this strong and this quickly on the heels of Fable doing strictly distillation," one expert told TechCrunch.

item.title: Google hit with $1 billion fine for breaking EU antitrust rules
item.description: The European Union has fined Google's parent company Alphabet €890 million (about $1 billion) for two separate violations of the bloc's Digital Markets Act (DMA). One penalty is for giving its own...

item.title: Google now lets you sign in to your account using a selfie video
item.description: Google announced yet another way to gain access to your account if you get locked out, forget your password, or don't have access to the phone, computer, or two-factor authentication apps you...

item.title: Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
item.description: Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video. The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing...

item.title: Framework’s premium laptop is shipping with less RAM
item.description: RAMageddon has claimed yet another victim, with Framework now announcing a "major and unfortunate" pricing update for its upcoming premium laptop - which also impacts folks who preordered it....

item.title: Remember Jibo? Its Successor Is a Wearable That Turns Your Life Into AI Slop
item.description: With “blessings” from the original Jibo founders, iKairos is a wearable or desk-mounted “AI journal” that turns your family moments into AI images and video.

item.title: The power line that could reshape New York’s grid is hitting snags
item.description: On July 3, as a heat wave swept the region, New York State’s grid imported 52 gigawatt-hours of electricity from Canada—enough to meet about 9% of its total electricity demand that day. Some of...

item.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
item.description: RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root...

item.title: Check Point Patches Exploited SmartConsole Flaw Allowing Full Admin Access
item.description: Check Point has released security updates to address multiple vulnerabilities impacting Security Management and Multi-Domain Management (MDSM) products, including a critical flaw that has come...

item.title: ServiceNow bets $40 million on Indian banking software specialist to expand its financial services push
item.description: ServiceNow's investment gives BusinessNext a strategic partner to expand its AI-powered banking software globally.
